lesson.menu_title: Conditionals I & II — If This, Then That
lesson.menu_desc: Talk about real and hypothetical situations — first conditional (if + present, will) for likely outcomes and second conditional (if + past, would) for imagined IT scenarios.
lesson.subtitle: Aula 18 — Conditionals I & II: If This, Then That
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Daniela Feitoza | Aula 18 -- Conditionals I & II

listenings.text: Hi Daniela, it is Sarah, your English coach. Today, listen to one short voice note about conditionals, the way we connect a condition with a result. There are two kinds you need at work. The first conditional is for real, likely situations. The form is, if plus present simple, then will plus the base verb. For example, if the server goes down, we will escalate the problem to the team. This is realistic, it can really happen. The second conditional is for imagined or unlikely situations. The form is, if plus past simple, then would plus the base verb. For example, if we had a bigger budget, we would migrate everything to the cloud. This is hypothetical, just an idea. Here is the key. In the second conditional we use the past form, but it is not really about the past. It is an imagined now. So remember, first conditional for real plans, will, and second conditional for hypothetical scenarios, would. Try it. Think about one real risk at work and one dream project, and make a sentence for each. Talk soon.

Hello Daniela, this is Marcus. I am leaving you a quick voicemail about the migration plan and the worst-case scenario. So, here is my thinking. If the test goes well this week, we will start the migration on Monday, that is the plan. But we need a backup plan too. If something breaks during the migration, we will roll back to the old system immediately, and I will escalate it to you and to the vendor. Now, honestly, if we had more downtime allowed, I would do the whole thing in one weekend, but we do not, so we have to do it in stages. And if I were you, I would tell the directors about the risk now, before we start, not after. One more thing. If the new platform did not depend on the old database, this would be so much easier, but it does, so we will prioritize the data first. Call me back when you can, and we will finalize the plan. Talk soon, Daniela.
